differences.title: 1. REATIVITY MODEL
differences.leftDesc: Re-renders the entire component tree for state changes.
differences.rightDesc: Directly updates DOM nodes with minimal overhead.

differences.title: 2. COMPILATION
differences.leftDesc: Transpiles JSX to JavaScript, often with runtime helpers.
differences.rightDesc: Compiles JSX into efficient, vanilla JavaScript.

differences.title: 4. STATE MANAGEMENT
differences.leftDesc: Uses hooks like `useState`, `useReducer` and Context API.

differences.title: 5. BUNDLE SIZE
differences.leftDesc: Includes a larger runtime for VDOM operations.
differences.rightDesc: Minimal runtime, highly optimized for size.

differences.title: 6. LEARNING CURVE
differences.leftDesc: More concepts to grasp initially (hooks, VDOM).
differences.rightDesc: Simpler core concepts, closer to vanilla JS.

differences.title: 7. PERFORMANCE
differences.leftDesc: Performance can be affected by VDOM diffing.
differences.rightDesc: Highly performant due to granular updates.
